seo.title: LLM Latency Optimization for AI Agents in Vercel AI SDK Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix llm latency optimization for ai agents in Vercel AI SDK agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Vercel AI SDK, LLM latency optimization issues for AI agents manifest as delayed tool calls, buffering in streaming responses, and edge function timeouts during complex reasoning chains. Agents built with `generateText` or `streamText` suffer from unoptimized prompt sizes and missing caching, leading to sluggish UIs and poor user experience. These problems are exacerbated by the framework's Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming model, where latency compounds across multiple provider calls.

howItManifests.symptom: Partial agent thoughts stream slowly, causing UI buffering
howItManifests.impact: Users abandon conversations after 3-5 second delays

howItManifests.symptom: Repeated API calls to same tools without memoization
howItManifests.impact: Edge functions hit 30s Vercel timeout limits

howItManifests.symptom: Initial token latency exceeds 2s due to 8k+ token prompts
howItManifests.impact: High bounce rates on agentic UEs

howItManifests.symptom: Large JSON tool responses block SSE stream
howItManifests.impact: Perceived hangs during structured output generation

detection.name: Stream Latency Profiler
detection.description: Measures time-to-first-token and token-per-second in `streamText` calls

detection.name: Prompt Token Counter
Tool Call Latency Monitor
detection.description: Tracks individual tool execution times in agent loops

fixes.name: Prompt Caching Middleware
fixes.description: Cache repeated system prompts to reduce initial latency

fixes.name: Compressed Streaming
fixes.description: Enable streaming with smaller chunk sizes and compression

fixes.name: Parallel Tool Execution

vexAutomation.heading: Automating LLM Latency Optimization for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically instruments Vercel AI SDK's `generateText`, `streamText`, and `streamObject` calls to measure time-to-first-token, tokens-per-second, and tool execution latency. It detects problematic patterns like oversized prompts (>8k tokens), slow tools (>1s execution), and buffering streams, then suggests targeted fixes including prompt compression, caching strategies, and model selection. Vex's middleware wraps your existing AI SDK calls without code changes, providing real-time latency dashboards and automated PRs with optimizations.

The SDK integration adds <1ms overhead while capturing 100% of LLM interactions. Vex correlates latency spikes with specific providers, prompt lengths, and tool combinations, enabling proactive optimization before production issues arise. Teams using Vex report 40-60% latency reductions across agentic applications.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex middleware in 2 minutes to cut agent latency by 50% with zero code changes
